focus: memory-write & async enrichment (transaction boundary, deferral/crash-safety, enrichment blast radius, exec-status collapse, E08x error classes, entity-density cache invalidation)
noveltyJustification: 3 new P2 findings this iteration (F-A2-01/02/03); cumulative-weighted basis = weightedNew(3) / weightedTotal(15: prior P1x2=10 + prior P2x2=2 + new P2x3=3) = 0.20. Per-iteration basis = 1.0 (all 3 findings new). Three P0-candidate hypotheses were refuted with file:line counterevidence rather than producing findings, which is honest non-novelty.

findingDetails.title: Background-enrichment inactive-row skip omits the 'archived' tier
findingDetails.evidence: Skip guard tests only `row.importance_tier === 'deprecated'`; search layer treats 'archived' as equally inactive (lib/search/hybrid-search.ts:2061 `importance_tier NOT IN ('deprecated','archived')`; lib/cognitive/attention-decay.ts:308). An archived row would pass the guard and run full runPostInsertEnrichment, repopulating graph/entity data the search layer hides. No server writer currently persists importance_tier='archived' (grep zero hits; tier-classifier.shouldArchive returns boolean only), so latent not live.
findingDetails.recommendation: Extend skip guard to `row.importance_tier === 'deprecated' || row.importance_tier === 'archived'` (or test against a shared inactive-tier set) so enrichment-active and search-active contracts agree.
findingDetails.scopeProof: declared A2 target handlers/memory-save.ts:2877; counterevidence in declared search-layer cross-ref

findingDetails.title: Stale line-number doc comment for entity-density cache-invalidation entry points
findingDetails.evidence: Comment cites 'memory-save.ts:2583 and memory-bulk-delete.ts:149,256'; actual save-path calls are memory-save.ts:2704 and :2882 (post-307-line churn the :2583 anchor no longer matches), and the comment omits mutation-hooks.ts:105, relation-backfill.ts:581, vector-index-mutations.ts:702/859 which also invalidate.
findingDetails.recommendation: Refresh the doc comment to enumerate the actual call sites, or remove the line-pinned breadcrumb.
affectedSurfaceHints: actual callers memory-save.ts:2704/2882, mutation-hooks.ts:105, relation-backfill.ts:581, vector-index-mutations.ts:702/859

findingDetails.title: classifySaveErrorCode over-broad substring routing for path-safety errors (E089)
findingDetails.evidence: E089 branch matches lower.includes('access denied')||'path must not contain'||'traversal'. 'access denied' is a generic substring that can appear in OS-level permission/ACL errors that are not validation failures, misclassifying them E089 (validation) and sending callers down the wrong recovery path. DB branch (511-520) is correctly ordered before it, but any message literally containing 'access denied' is captured by the validation branch.
findingDetails.recommendation: Tighten path-safety matches to anchored/contextual tokens (e.g. require a path-safety prefix) or add a permission/EACCES branch before E089 so OS permission errors do not collapse into validation.
